execution: Manual edit of data/url-inventory/blogs.json to add the 3 new pillar articles published earlier this session (PR #202). The url-inventory file is the source of truth for app/sitemap.ts; without these entries the new articles wouldn't appear in /sitemap.xml until the next refresh-inventory workflow run.
audit_finding: The 3 pillar articles created in PR #202 (best-mattress-los-angeles, mattress-store-near-me-los-angeles, mattress-financing-options-los-angeles) were not in data/url-inventory/blogs.json. The url-inventory snapshot is generated by scripts/refresh-inventory.yml / pull-articles-via-storefront.mjs and was last refreshed before the new articles existed. app/sitemap.ts reads from this snapshot, so /sitemap.xml as currently deployed does NOT include the 3 new article URLs.
verification: npx next build succeeds. Article count on mattress-buying-guide blog went from 605 to 608 in the inventory. The sitemap is statically prerendered (○ icon in build output) so the next production deploy will include the 3 new article URLs.
side_finding: Inventory has stale entries for several other recent articles (e.g. best-mattress-for-back-pain, where-to-buy-a-mattress-in-los-angeles, when-is-the-best-time-to-buy-a-mattress) where `isPublished` is `false` and `publishedAt` is `null`, but these articles ARE published in production. The inventory snapshot is stale on those entries. Not touching here (out of scope for this fix); a refresh-inventory run would clean it up.
seo_impact: Google's Search Console will discover the 3 article URLs via the next sitemap crawl (typically 1-3 days after deployment). Manual URL Inspection submission (user task) accelerates this to minutes.
